data.name: ConfirmDeleteFile
data.value: Delete file "{0}"? This cannot be undone.

data.name: ConfirmDeleteFolder
data.value: Delete folder "{0}" and all of its contents? This cannot be undone.

data.name: ConfirmDeleteMultiple
data.value: Delete the {0} selected items? This cannot be undone.

data.name: Sftp_FileTypeExt
data.comment: 文件浏览器“类型”列:{0} 为大写扩展名,如 "PHP File"。

data.name: Auth_FingerprintFirstConnect
data.value: Fingerprint: first connection, recorded during handshake

data.name: Boot_AlreadyRunning
data.value:
VelaShell is already running.
Please switch to the open window; only one instance can run at a time.

data.name: Boot_StartupFailed
Diag_AllPassed
data.value: No issues found — all checks passed; you can connect normally.

data.name: DiagSvc_AuthFailedDesc
data.value: The server rejected the login for {0}: {1}

data.name: DiagSvc_JumpChainFailed
data.value: Failed to establish the jump chain

data.name: DiagSvc_JumpChainLoop
data.value: Jump host profiles form a circular reference; check each profile's jump host setting.

data.name: DiagSvc_JumpChainTooLong
data.value: The jump chain exceeds 5 hops; please simplify the jump levels.

data.name: DiagSvc_NoBannerDesc
data.value: {0}:{1} is reachable but returned no SSH identification within {2} seconds.

data.name: DiagSvc_NoCredsSkip
data.value: No saved credentials; authentication test skipped

data.name: DiagSvc_NotSshPort
data.value: Port is not running an SSH service

data.name: DiagSvc_SshBannerReadError
data.value: Error reading the SSH identification string: {0}

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestCheckCreds
data.value: Check that the username and password/private key are correct; mind the letter case.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestCheckDns
data.value: Check your local DNS settings, or connect using the IP address instead.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestCheckHostname
data.value: Check that the hostname is spelled correctly.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestCheckPort
data.value: Confirm port {0} is correct and the SSH service is running on the server.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestCheckSshd
data.value: Network and port are fine; the failure happened during SSH negotiation. Check the server's sshd log (/var/log/auth.log) for details.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestDefaultPort
data.value: Verify the port number: SSH defaults to 22; currently configured as {0}.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestDiagnoseJump
data.value: Run connection diagnostics on the jump host itself first to locate the failing hop.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestFirewall
data.value: Check that the server firewall or cloud security group allows this port.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestHostOnline
data.value: Confirm the host is online (try ping or access it from another network).

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestJumpCreds
data.value: Jump host profiles need saved credentials to connect without prompts; check each jump host profile.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestKeyMatch
data.value: Confirm the private key matches the public key on the server and the passphrase is correct.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestMitm
data.value: If this change is unexpected, beware of a man-in-the-middle attack and verify with the server administrator first.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestPasswordDisabled
data.value: The server may have password login disabled (PasswordAuthentication no); try key authentication instead.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestPortOccupied
data.value: The port may be used by another service (HTTP, database, etc.); check the server configuration.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestRecreateJump
data.value: If the jump host profile was deleted, connect directly or recreate the jump host profile.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestReselectJump
data.value: Open this connection's edit dialog → Advanced options and reselect the jump host.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestSaveCreds
data.value: This profile has no saved password/key, so a login prompt appears on connect; to run the authentication test automatically, save credentials in the edit dialog.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestTrustNewKey
data.value: The server host key differs from the local record: if the server really was reinstalled, trust the new fingerprint when prompted.

data.name: DiagSvc_SuggestVpn
data.value: If the target is on an internal network, make sure you are on the corporate network or VPN.

data.name: Editor_SavedStatus
data.value: Saved and synced to the server · {0}

data.name: Editor_UnsavedBody
data.value: The file has unsaved changes; closing will discard them.

data.name: HostKey_TrustOnceTip
data.value: Allow this connection without saving to trusted hosts; you will be asked again after the app restarts

data.name: HostKey_TrustPermanentlyTip
data.value: Record this fingerprint and stop asking on future connections; remove it in Settings → Security Audit → Trusted Hosts

data.name: KeySvc_AlertChangedAccepted
data.value: Host fingerprint of {0} changed; the user confirmed permanent trust of the new fingerprint: {1}

data.name: KeySvc_AlertChangedBlocked
data.value: Host fingerprint of {0} does not match the known_hosts record; the connection was blocked (possible man-in-the-middle). New fingerprint: {1}

data.name: KeySvc_AlertChangedTrustOnce
data.value: Host fingerprint of {0} changed; the user chose to trust it this time only (not saved): {1}

data.name: KeySvc_AlertFirstTrustOnce
data.value: The user chose to trust the first-connection fingerprint of {0} this time only (not saved): {1}

data.name: Main_CloseConfirmBody
data.value: Active SSH sessions are still running; closing the window will disconnect them all. Quit anyway?

data.name: Main_PasteMultilineBody
Msg_AuthFailed
data.value: Authentication failed: incorrect username, password or key for {0}.

data.name: Msg_AuthPromptCancelled
data.value: No saved credentials for this profile; login verification was cancelled.

data.name: Msg_AutoRecordEnabledHint
data.value: Auto recording enabled; applies to connections established from now on.

data.name: Msg_BackgroundConnectorNotConfigured
data.value: Background connection service is not configured.

data.name: Msg_CloudRevisionCount
data.value: {0} versions in the cloud (showing up to 30).

data.name: Msg_ConnectFailed
data.value: Connection failed: could not establish an SSH session with {0}.

data.name: Msg_ConnectTimeout
data.value: Connection timed out: {0} did not respond.

data.name: Msg_DefaultEditorNotConfigured
data.value: No default editor configured. Set one under Settings → File Transfer → Default Editor (e.g. notepad).

data.name: Msg_FileTooLargeForBuiltInEditor
data.value: The file exceeds 5 MB; the built-in editor is for small text files only. Download it and edit locally.

data.name: Msg_KeyAlreadyExists
data.value: A key with the same name already exists; not imported.

data.name: Msg_LocalPortInUse
data.value: Local port {0} is already in use; choose another port.

data.name: Msg_NetworkError
data.value: Network error: cannot reach {0}. Check the host and port.

data.name: Msg_NoCloudRevisions
data.value: No versions in the cloud yet.

data.name: Msg_NoRecordings
data.value: No recordings yet. Enable session recording under Settings → Security & Audit to record the output of new connections.

data.name: Msg_ProxyError
data.value: Proxy error: cannot connect to {0} through the proxy.

data.name: Msg_RecordingEmpty
data.value: This recording has no data chunks (there may have been no output while recording).

data.name: Msg_SelectRecordingToPlay
data.value: Select a recording on the left to start playback

data.name: Msg_ServerConnectionUnavailable
data.value: The connection to the server is unavailable; please retry (it will reconnect automatically).

data.name: Msg_SshConnectionLostDetail
data.value: // The SSH connection to {0} was lost

data.name: Msg_SshServiceNotConfigured
data.value: SSH connection service is not configured.

data.name: Msg_SyncConfigSavedE2eOff
data.value: Sync settings saved (end-to-end encryption: disabled; credentials are never uploaded).

data.name: Msg_TunnelConnectedBackground
data.value: Connected • background tunnel channel (independent of terminal sessions)

data.name: Msg_TunnelNotConnectedAuto
data.value: Not connected • connects automatically when a tunnel is created or started

data.name: Msg_UpdateServiceNotAvailable
data.value: Auto-update is not available yet; please get new releases from the project releases page.

data.name: SetAbout_UpdateAvailableManual
data.value: Update {0} is available, but this install location can't be updated automatically (folder isn't writable). Please download the latest release from the project page.

data.name: SetAbout_DownloadFailed
data.value: Download failed. Please try again later.

data.name: SetAbout_UpdateCheckFailed
data.value: Couldn't check for updates. Please try again later.

data.name: SetAbout_UpdateReady
data.value: Update {0} is ready. Restart to finish installing.

data.name: SetAbout_ApplyFailed
data.value: Couldn't apply the update; the previous version has been restored. Please try again or download manually.

data.name: Palette_SearchPlaceholder
data.value: Type a command or search sessions…

data.name: Profile_GroupPlaceholder
data.value: Select or enter a new group name

data.name: Profile_JumpHostHint
data.value: // Connects to the jump host first, then reaches this host via its port forwarding; the jump host must have saved credentials, and a jump host may use another jump host (multi-hop chain)

data.name: Recorder_AutoRecordTip
data.value: Same as Settings → Security Audit → Session Recording; applies to connections opened afterwards

data.name: Recorder_ExportTip
data.value: Export as asciicast v2 (.cast), playable with asciinema and similar tools

data.name: Recorder_MaximizeTip
data.value: Maximize / restore (double-click the title bar also works)

data.name: Recorder_Subtitle
data.value: // Terminal auditing, search, and timeline playback

data.name: SetAbout_FreeNotice
data.value: This software is free forever; the only official channel is GitHub Releases — any "paid version" is pirated

data.name: SetAbout_LicenseNotice
data.value: Dual-licensed: AGPL-3.0 open source / commercial license (see LICENSE-COMMERCIAL.md in the repository)

data.name: SetAbout_OpenSourceDepsDesc
data.value: // VelaShell is built on these excellent open source projects

data.name: SetAppear_AccentColorDesc
data.value: // Application accent color (leave empty for theme default)

data.name: SetAppear_Ansi16Desc
data.value: // Standard 16-color palette used in the terminal

data.name: SetAppear_ColorSchemeDesc
data.value: // Apply a complete terminal color scheme in one click; by default follows the app theme: dark = Dracula, light = Solarized Light

data.name: SetAppear_SelectionColorDesc
data.value: // Background color of selected text in the terminal

data.name: SetAppear_SidebarPositionDesc
data.value: // Show the sidebar on the left or right side of the window

data.name: SetAppear_Subtitle
data.value: // Customize the visual style of the application

data.name: SetAppear_ThemeModeDesc
data.value: // Choose light, dark, or follow system

data.name: SetAppear_UiFontDesc
data.value: // Font for menus, tabs and other UI elements

data.name: SetAppear_UiFontSizeDesc
data.value: // Default font size for UI elements

data.name: SetAppear_WindowOpacityDesc
data.value: // Adjust the transparency of the window background

data.name: SetAppear_WindowStateDesc
data.value: // Window size behavior when the app starts

data.name: SetDonate_CoffeeDesc
data.value: // Donations are entirely voluntary, any amount welcome; all VelaShell features stay free forever either way

data.name: SetDonate_ContributeBody
data.value: More valuable than donations is your participation: submit PRs, improve performance and UX, fix bugs, polish the docs, or share problems and ideas in Issues. Every code contribution takes VelaShell further.

data.name: SetDonate_ContributeTitle
data.value: The best way to support: contribute

data.name: SetDonate_InternationalDesc
data.value: // Outside China, or prefer to support in USD, EUR or another currency? You can pay via Wise

data.name: SetDonate_Intro
data.value: VelaShell is an open source project maintained by an individual. If it makes your daily ops and development smoother, feel free to support it in whatever way you like — every bit of support turns into new features, performance improvements, and faster bug fixes.

data.name: SetDonate_Thanks
data.value: Thank you for reading this far. Whether it's a donation, a star, an issue, or a PR — every one of them keeps this project going.

data.name: SetDonate_WiseHint
data.value: Scan the QR code or click the payment link below to donate in your local currency:

data.name: SetGeneral_AutoReconnectDesc
data.value: // Automatically try to reconnect after an unexpected disconnect (manual disconnects are not retried)

data.name: SetGeneral_ClearHistoryConfirm
data.value: This will clear command history and recent connections. This action cannot be undone. Continue?

data.name: SetGeneral_ClearHistoryDesc
data.value: // Clear command history and recent connections

data.name: SetGeneral_ConfirmBeforeCloseDesc
data.value: // Ask before closing the window when sessions are connected; with "Minimize to tray" on, the close button only hides the window and skips this confirmation

data.name: SetGeneral_ImportExportDesc
data.value: // Backs up or migrates app settings only; connections, groups, keys and snippets are not included

data.name: SetGeneral_LaunchAtStartupDesc
data.value: // Run VelaShell automatically when the system starts

data.name: SetGeneral_LogRetentionDaysDesc
data.value: // Terminal session logs older than this are cleaned up automatically; file transfer log retention is set separately on the File Transfer page

data.name: SetGeneral_MaxRetriesDesc
data.value: // Maximum number of automatic reconnect attempts; unrelated to file transfer retries

data.name: SetGeneral_MinimizeToTrayDesc
data.value: // Hide to the system tray when the close button is clicked; quit only via the tray menu "Exit"

data.name: SetGeneral_NotifyOnDisconnectDesc
data.value: // Alert on the status bar and tab when an SSH connection drops unexpectedly

data.name: SetGeneral_RememberPasswordsDesc
data.value: // Save connection passwords to encrypted local storage

data.name: SetGeneral_SessionLoggingDesc
data.value: // Automatically record terminal output for all sessions

data.name: SetGeneral_SoundOnDisconnectDesc
data.value: // Play the system sound when an SSH connection drops unexpectedly; security event sounds are set separately on the Security Audit page

data.name: SetKeys_DefaultAuthKeyDesc
data.value: // Key used by default for new connections

data.name: SetKeys_StorageHint
data.value: Keys are stored under ~/.ssh/; click the copy button to copy the public key

data.name: SetKeys_Subtitle
data.value: // Manage SSH key pairs and certificates

data.name: SetSecurity_AlertSound
data.value: Play a sound on security events

data.name: SetSecurity_AlertSoundDesc
data.value: // Play the system sound on security events such as host fingerprint changes; the disconnect sound is configured separately on the General page

data.name: SetSecurity_AutoRecordDesc
data.value: // Record terminal output to a local database for audit and replay; applies to connections opened afterwards, retention follows General → Terminal session log retention

data.name: SetSecurity_BlockOnChange
data.value: Block and alert on fingerprint change

data.name: SetSecurity_BlockOnChangeDesc
data.value: // Refuse the connection if it doesn't match the known_hosts record, preventing man-in-the-middle attacks

data.name: SetSecurity_ConfirmFirstFingerprint
data.value: Require manual fingerprint confirmation on first connect

data.name: SetSecurity_ConfirmFirstFingerprintDesc
data.value: // When off, the fingerprint is recorded automatically on first connect (TOFU)

data.name: SetSecurity_KnownHostsDesc
data.value: // Hosts with recorded fingerprints; after deletion, the next connection runs first-time fingerprint confirmation again

data.name: SetSecurity_MaskAddressesDesc
data.value: // Anti-leak for screenshots: hides IP and port; defaults to on each time Settings opens, and the off state is not saved

data.name: SetSecurity_RecordingCenterDesc
data.value: // Browse recordings, replay on a timeline (speed control and idle skipping), export, and delete

data.name: SetSecurity_SectionAlerts
data.value: Alert channels: in-app notification + sound + Webhook

data.name: SetSecurity_Subtitle
data.value: // Recording policy, trust policy, and risk alerts

data.name: SetSecurity_WebhookDesc
data.value: // Push security events as JSON POST to a custom HTTP endpoint

data.name: SetSecurity_WebhookPayloadDesc
data.value: // Event payload: { source, event, message, detail, timestamp }; an unreachable endpoint never affects the connection flow

data.name: SetShortcuts_Subtitle
data.value: // Built-in key bindings in this version (read-only)

data.name: SetSnippets_Intro
data.value: Create frequently used command snippets, bind them to shortcuts, or assign them to specific sessions.

data.name: SetSnippets_ShowInSidebar
data.value: Show quick commands in the sidebar

data.name: SetSnippets_ShowInSidebarDesc
data.value: Display a collapsible quick command panel in the left sidebar.

data.name: SetSnippets_Subtitle
data.value: // Common command snippets, runnable from the quick command panel

data.name: SetSync_AutoSyncDesc
data.value: // Pull automatically at startup; push automatically after saving settings (a new version is created only when something changed)

data.name: SetSync_DeviceNameDesc
data.value: // Written into sync metadata so version history shows which device made each change

data.name: SetSync_E2EDesc
data.value: // With a sync passphrase set, data is encrypted locally with AES-256-GCM before upload, so GitHub only sees ciphertext, and only then are connection passwords included in the payload; without a passphrase, passwords and key passphrases are never uploaded. All devices must enter the same passphrase.

data.name: SetSync_E2EEnabledNotice
data.value: ✓ End-to-end encryption is on: this Gist's data is ciphertext and the passphrase is stored encrypted on this device; other devices must enter the same passphrase, and entering a new one and saving the configuration replaces it

data.name: SetSync_EnableDesc
data.value: // When off, no automatic or manual sync will run

data.name: SetSync_GistIdDesc
data.value: // Leave blank to auto-create on the first push; enter the same Id on other devices to link them

data.name: SetSync_GistIdPlaceholder
data.value: Filled automatically after the first push

data.name: SetSync_HistoryDesc
data.value: // Every sync is a Gist revision; restoring an old version pushes it as a new one, so history is never lost

data.name: SetSync_Intro
data.value: Sync data is stored in a secret Gist under your own GitHub account, and every sync creates a restorable history version. Multiple devices on the same account sync seamlessly by using the same token and Gist Id.

data.name: SetSync_PassphrasePlaceholder
data.value: Sync passphrase (leave blank to keep the saved one)

data.name: SetSync_PullOnlyTip
data.value: Force-apply the latest cloud version to this device

data.name: SetSync_PushOnlyTip
data.value: Force-upload local data as the latest cloud version

data.name: SetSync_ScopeProfilesDesc
data.value: // Server connections, groups, and port-forwarding tunnels; connections that exist only locally are never deleted by the cloud

data.name: SetSync_Subtitle
data.value: // Sync your configuration across devices via GitHub Gist (with version history)

data.name: SetSync_SyncNowTip
data.value: Direction is chosen automatically: push if local is newer, pull if the cloud is newer; if both changed, the newer side wins (older data can be recovered from version history)

data.name: SetSync_TokenDesc
data.value: // Create it under GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens with only the gist scope; the token is encrypted with a local key and never uploaded

data.name: SetSync_TokenGuideStep1
data.value: 1. Click "Create token" below to open the GitHub token page (the required gist scope is pre-selected; sign in to GitHub first);

data.name: SetSync_TokenGuideStep2
data.value: 2. Set a note and expiration as needed, then click Generate token;

data.name: SetSync_TokenGuideStep3
data.value: 3. Copy the token starting with ghp_, paste it into the box below, and save the configuration.

data.name: SetSync_TokenGuideTitle
data.value: How do I get a GitHub token?

data.name: SetSync_TokenLocalOnly
data.value: The token is stored encrypted on this device only and is never uploaded with sync data.

data.name: SetSync_TokenPlaceholder
data.value: ghp_… (leave blank to keep the saved token)

data.name: SetTerm_BellBehaviorDesc
data.value: // When a remote program sends BEL: play the system sound, do nothing, or flash the screen

data.name: SetTerm_ConfirmMultilinePasteDesc
data.value: // Ask for confirmation when pasting content that contains line breaks

data.name: SetTerm_CopyOnSelectDesc
data.value: // On: selection is copied automatically on mouse release or word double-click; Off: selection is not copied, use Ctrl+Shift+C to copy manually

data.name: SetTerm_CtrlCCopyDesc
data.value: // On: with text selected, Ctrl+C copies it instead of sending an interrupt (still interrupts when nothing is selected); Off: Ctrl+C always sends the interrupt signal ^C

data.name: SetTerm_FontDesc
data.value: // Monospaced font used in the terminal

data.name: SetTerm_ImeSupportDesc
data.value: // Enable inline input for Chinese, Japanese and other IMEs

data.name: SetTerm_AutoOpenSftpDesc
data.value: Decides whether the SFTP file browser opens when a new remote session connects. Each tab then remembers its own show/hide state independently.

data.name: SetTerm_OutputEncodingDesc
data.value: // Character encoding used to decode remote output

data.name: SetTerm_RightClickPasteDesc
data.value: // Paste clipboard contents on right-click in the terminal

data.name: SetTerm_ScrollbackDesc
data.value: // Maximum number of lines kept in scrollback

data.name: SetTerm_ShowLineTimestampDesc
data.value: Show each line's receive time (HH:mm:ss) in a left gutter.

data.name: SetTerm_ShowLineNumberDesc
data.value: Show each line's buffer line number in a left gutter.

data.name: SetTerm_ShowFoldMarkerDesc
data.value: Show a fold column to collapse earlier output into expandable regions.

data.name: SetTerm_GutterBlankDesc
data.value: Add a small blank gap between the gutter and the output.

data.name: SetTerm_ScrollOnOutputDesc
data.value: // Jump to the bottom on new output while viewing history; off keeps the current position

data.name: SetTerm_StartupCommandDesc
data.value: // Runs silently after connect/reconnect (not echoed in the terminal), appended after the built-in prompt-fix script; leave empty to run only the built-in script

data.name: SetTerm_TabFlashAlertDesc
data.value: // Flash the tab when an inactive tab receives a bell

data.name: SetTerm_TermTypeDesc
data.value: // Terminal capability profile advertised to the remote host

data.name: Settings_ResetConfirmMessage
data.value: This will restore all settings on every page to factory defaults (written to disk only after you click "Save Settings"). Continue?

data.name: SetTransfer_BandwidthEnableDesc
data.value: // Cap the maximum bandwidth used by file transfers

data.name: SetTransfer_DefaultEditorDesc
data.value: Windows: full path or command name; Linux: command name; macOS: app name

data.name: SetTransfer_DownloadDirDesc
data.value: Default save location for downloaded files

data.name: SetTransfer_FileExistsDesc
data.value: What to do when the destination already has a file with the same name

data.name: SetTransfer_LogRetentionDaysDesc
data.value: // Transfer logs older than this are cleaned up automatically; terminal session log retention is configured separately on the General page

data.name: SetTransfer_MaxConcurrentDesc
data.value: Number of file transfer tasks running at the same time

data.name: SetTransfer_NotifyOnCompleteDesc
data.value: // Show a notification when an upload/download finishes; disconnect notifications are configured separately on the General page

data.name: SetTransfer_ShowHiddenDesc
data.value: // Whether the file browser lists dot-files; the toolbar toggle is saved back to this setting

data.name: SetTransfer_Subtitle
data.value: // Configure SFTP and file transfer behavior

data.name: SetTransfer_TransferLoggingDesc
data.value: // Keep a detailed log of file transfers

data.name: Sftp_LocalOverwriteBody
Sftp_NoEditorBody
data.value:
"Open with default editor" needs an editor program to be set first: On Windows enter the full exe path or a command name (e.g. notepad); on Linux a command name (e.g. gedit); on macOS an app name (e.g. Visual Studio Code).
Open Settings now to configure it?

data.name: Sftp_RemoteOverwriteBody
Svc_JumpChainLoop
data.value: Jump host profiles form a circular reference; check the jump host settings.

data.name: Svc_JumpChainTooLong
data.value: Jump chain is too long (5 hops max).

data.name: Svc_JumpHostMissing
data.value: The jump host profile no longer exists (it may have been deleted); please set it again.

data.name: Svc_PrivateKeyRequired
data.value: Private key authentication requires a private key path.

data.name: SyncSvc_BothChangedLocalWins
data.value: (both sides changed; the local copy is newer and was pushed — the old cloud version remains in history)

data.name: SyncSvc_BothChangedRemoteWins
data.value: (both sides changed; the cloud copy is newer and was applied — old local data can be recovered from version history)

data.name: SyncSvc_Forbidden
data.value: Request rejected by GitHub (possibly rate-limited; try again later).

data.name: SyncSvc_GistNotFound
data.value: The Gist does not exist or the token has no access (check the Gist Id and token account).

data.name: SyncSvc_MissingPayload
data.value: Cloud data is missing its payload.

data.name: SyncSvc_MissingRawUrl
data.value: The Gist file is missing raw_url.

data.name: SyncSvc_NeedPassphrase
data.value: Cloud data is end-to-end encrypted: enter the same sync passphrase on this device before pulling.

data.name: SyncSvc_NoGistBound
data.value: No Gist is linked yet: push once first, or enter the Gist Id created on another device.

data.name: SyncSvc_NoGistId
data.value: GitHub did not return a Gist Id.

data.name: SyncSvc_NoGistNoRestore
data.value: No Gist is linked; there are no versions to restore.

data.name: SyncSvc_NoRemoteFile
data.value: The cloud Gist contains no sync data file.

data.name: SyncSvc_NoToken
data.value: No GitHub token configured (gist scope required).

data.name: SyncSvc_RestoredAndPushed
data.value: Restored to revision {0} and pushed as the latest version.

data.name: SyncSvc_RevisionNoFile
data.value: This revision contains no sync data file.

data.name: SyncSvc_TokenDecryptFailed
data.value: Could not decrypt the stored GitHub token; please enter it again.

data.name: SyncSvc_TokenInvalid
data.value: The GitHub token is invalid or expired; regenerate it (gist scope required).

data.name: SyncSvc_UpToDate
data.value: Local and cloud are already in sync; nothing to do.

data.name: SyncSvc_WrongPassphrase
data.value: Incorrect sync passphrase; cannot decrypt the cloud data.

data.name: Tunnel_DynamicSocksTip
data.value: Local port acts as a SOCKS5 proxy for any target (ssh -D)

data.name: Tunnel_EmptyHint
data.value: // No tunnels on this server yet — create one below

data.name: Tunnel_LocalForwardTip
data.value: Forward a local port to a target service via the server (ssh -L)

data.name: Tunnel_RemoteForwardTip
data.value: Forward a server port back to a local service (ssh -R)

data.name: Tunnel_StartTip
data.value: Start tunnel (connects to the server automatically)

data.name: TunnelSvc_ForwardProhibited
data.value: The server prohibits port forwarding (sshd setting AllowTcpForwarding no).

data.name: TunnelSvc_TargetRefused
data.value: Target refused the connection: the target address is resolved from the server's perspective — use 127.0.0.1 to forward a service on the server itself, and confirm the target port is listening.

data.name: TunnelSvc_TargetUnreachable
data.value: Target unreachable: confirm the target host is reachable from the server (internal address / firewall).

data.name: SetGeneral_FollowActiveTerminalDesc
data.value: When switching terminal tabs, reveal and select the matching saved connection.

data.name: ZModem_ChooseDownloadFolderRetry
data.value: No folder chosen — choose one, or cancel again to abort the transfer

data.name: ZModem_ChooseUploadFilesRetry
data.value: No files chosen — choose some, or cancel again to abort the transfer

data.name: QuickCmd_RecentlyModified
data.value: Files modified in the last hour
